avalancheActivity.comment: Avalanche danger above the treeline is considerable, below that altitude danger is low. Fresh snowdrift accumulations are easily triggered. Slabs can be triggered by minimum additional loading and grow to medium-sized releases. Settling noises when treading the snowpack and fractures on the surface can indicate imminent danger. Danger zones occur in wind-loaded steep terrain, behind sharp drops in the landscape, in gullies and bowls. The snowdrift accumulations generated on Sunday are blanketed by fresher snow, thus impossible to recognize. On very steep sunny grass-covered slopes, glide-snow avalanches can slide away and grow to medium size.

snowpackStructure.comment: The fresh snow and snowdrifts from Wednesday and Thursday have been able to settle and consolidate somewhat. On steep sunny slopes the fresh snow is already slightly moist and encrusted. As a result of northerly winds, 5-15cm of fresh snow was registered on Sunday, generating mostly small-sized trigger-sensitive snowdrift accumulations. These will expand further on Monday daytime due to strong NW winds and fresh snowfall. In the uppermost third of the snowpack are weak intermediate layers and graupel. The old snowpack is generally well consolidated and compact.

tendency.highlights: As a result of fresh snow and wind, avalanche danger will increase on Monday night.

weatherForecast.comment: Shifting weather. As a result of strong-velocity NW winds, cloud cover will more in, snowfall will set in as of midday everywhere. In the evening and on Monday night, persistent snowfall is anticipated, intermittently heavy. Temperature at 2000m: from -7 degrees; at 3000m: -14 degrees. Strong NW winds at high altitude.

avalancheActivity.comment: Avalanche danger above the treeline is considerable, below that altitude danger is low. Fresh snowdrift accumulations are easily triggered. Slabs can be triggered by minimum additional loading and grow to medium-sized releases. Settling noises when treading the snowpack and fractures on the surface can indicate imminent danger. Danger zones occur in wind-loaded steep terrain, behind sharp drops in the landscape, in gullies and bowls. The snowdrift accumulations generated on Sunday are blanketed by fresher snow, thus impossible to recognize. On very steep sunny grass-covered slopes, glide-snow avalanches can slide away and grow to medium size.

snowpackStructure.comment: The fresh snow and snowdrifts from Wednesday and Thursday have been able to settle and consolidate somewhat. On steep sunny slopes the fresh snow is already slightly moist and encrusted. As a result of northerly winds, 5-15cm of fresh snow was registered on Sunday, generating mostly small-sized trigger-sensitive snowdrift accumulations. These will expand further on Monday daytime due to strong NW winds and fresh snowfall. In the uppermost third of the snowpack are weak intermediate layers and graupel. The old snowpack is generally well consolidated and compact. On high-altitude shady slopes these layers cover a poorly-structured old snowpack fundament.

tendency.highlights: As a result of fresh snow and wind, avalanche danger will increase significantly on Monday night.

weatherForecast.comment: Shifting weather. As a result of strong-velocity NW winds, cloud cover will more in, snowfall will set in as of midday everywhere. In the evening and on Monday night, persistent snowfall is anticipated, intermittently heavy. Temperature at 2000m: from -7 degrees; at 3000m: -14 degrees. Strong NW winds at high altitude.

ALBINA.generalHeadline: Main problem: fresh snowdrifts above timberline
